heading: Restrictions on recycled oil
label: 6935 Restrictions on recycled oil
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to .section 6932 of this title

Amendments1984—Subsec. (a). , §§ 241(a), 242, designated existing provisions as subsec. (a) and inserted “, consistent with the protection of human health and the environment” at end.Pub. L. 98–616Subsecs. (b) to (d). , added subsecs. (b) to (d).Pub. L. 98–616, § 241(a)

Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of certain enforcement functions of Administrator or other official of Environmental Protection Agency under this chapter to Federal Inspector, Office of Federal Inspector for the Alaska Natural Gas Transportation System, and subsequent transfer to Secretary of Energy, then to Federal Coordinator for Alaska Natural Gas Transportation Projects, see note set out under .section 6903 of this title
amendments: Amendments1984—Subsec. (a). , §§ 241(a), 242, designated existing provisions as subsec. (a) and inserted “, consistent with the protection of human health and the environment” at end.Pub. L. 98–616Subsecs. (b) to (d). , added subsecs. (b) to (d).Pub. L. 98–616, § 241(a)

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Not later than one year after , the Administrator shall promulgate regulations establishing such performance standards and other requirements as may be necessary to protect the public health and the environment from hazards associated with recycled oil. In developing such regulations, the Administrator shall conduct an analysis of the economic impact of the regulations on the oil recycling industry. The Administrator shall ensure that such regulations do not discourage the recovery or recycling of used oil, consistent with the protection of human health and the environment.October 15, 1980

children.heading: Identification or listing of used oil as hazardous waste
children.label: b Identification or listing of used oil as hazardous waste
children.text: Not later than twelve months after , the Administrator shall propose whether to list or identify used automobile and truck crankcase oil as hazardous waste under . Not later than twenty-four months after , the Administrator shall make a final determination whether to list or identify used automobile and truck crankcase oil and other used oil as hazardous wastes under .November 8, 1984November 8, 1984section 6921 of this titlesection 6921 of this title

children.heading: Used oil which is recycled
children.label: c Used oil which is recycled

children.text: With respect to generators and transporters of used oil identified or listed as a hazardous waste under , the standards promulgated under section 6921(d), 6922, and 6923 of this title shall not apply to such used oil if such used oil is recycled.section 6921 of this title1So in original. Probably should be “sections”.1

children.label: A
children.text: In the case of used oil which is exempt under paragraph (1), not later than twenty-four months after , the Administrator shall promulgate such standards under this subsection regarding the generation and transportation of used oil which is recycled as may be necessary to protect human health and the environment. In promulgating such regulations with respect to generators, the Administrator shall take into account the effect of such regulations on environmentally acceptable types of used oil recycling and the effect of such regulations on small quantity generators and generators which are small businesses (as defined by the Administrator).November 8, 1984

children.label: I
children.text: enters into an agreement or other arrangement (including an agreement or arrangement with an independent transporter or with an agent of the recycler) for delivery of such used oil to a recycling facility which has a permit under (or for which a valid permit is deemed to be in effect under subsection (d)), orsection 6925(c) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: recycles such used oil at one or more facilities of the generator which has such a permit under (or for which a valid permit is deemed to have been issued under subsection (d) of this section);section 6925 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: such used oil is not mixed by the generator with other types of hazardous wastes; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the generator maintains such records relating to such used oil, including records of agreements or other arrangements for delivery of such used oil to any recycling facility referred to in clause (i)(I), as the Administrator deems necessary to protect human health and the environment.

The regulations under this subsection regarding the transportation of used oil which is exempt from the standards promulgated under section 6921(d), 6922, and 6923 of this title under paragraph (1) shall require the transporters of such used oil to deliver such used oil to a facility which has a valid permit under or which is deemed to have a valid permit under subsection (d) of this section. The Administrator shall also establish other standards for such transporters as may be necessary to protect human health and the environment.1section 6925 of this title

The owner or operator of a facility which recycles used oil which is exempt under subsection (c)(1), shall be deemed to have a permit under this subsection for all such treatment or recycling (and any associated tank or container storage) if such owner and operator comply with standards promulgated by the Administrator under ; except that the Administrator may require such owners and operators to obtain an individual permit under if he determines that an individual permit is necessary to protect human health and the environment.section 6924 of this titlesection 6925(c) of this title

Notwithstanding any other provision of law, any generator who recycles used oil which is exempt under subsection (c)(1) shall not be required to obtain a permit under with respect to such used oil until the Administrator has promulgated standards under regarding the recycling of such used oil.section 6925(c) of this titlesection 6924 of this title
